question: Consider a classical antiferro quadrupole rotor model on the diamond lattice. Each site carries a two-component unit quadrupole vector Q = (Q_u, Q_v) with |Q|=1 (plane-rotor / XY-like). The nearest-neighbor (NN) interaction on the diamond lattice is antiferromagnetic with coupling strength J=1 (energy unit). Each site also has a single-site threefold cubic anisotropy term with parameter c=3.0. The Hamiltonian is H = J sum_{<ij>} Q_i . Q_j - c sum_i cos(3 * phi_i) where phi_i is the polar angle of Q_i, and the first sum is over NN diamond-lattice bonds. No external field (h=0). Systems are simulated on cubic periodic-boundary cells at multiple system sizes. Using Monte Carlo with Binder-ratio and correlation-length crossing analyses, determine the critical temperature T_c of the zero-field ordering transition in units of J/k_B (quantity: kBTc_over_J). Output a JSON object {"kBTc_over_J": <value>}.

metadata.system: Classical antiferro quadrupole rotor (plane-rotor, |Q|=1) on diamond lattice, AF coupling J=1, c=3.0, h=0
metadata.source_excerpt: For the antiferro rotor model on the diamond lattice with nearest-neighbor antiferro coupling J=1 (energy unit) and threefold single-site anisotropy parameter c=3.0, Monte Carlo Binder-ratio and correlation-length crossing analyses on cubic systems with periodic boundary conditions yield a critical temperature estimate T_c=1.2695(3)
metadata.difficulty_note: Non-standard lattice (diamond); 3D XY universality class; threefold anisotropy breaks O(2) to Z_3 symmetry
